content: I'm looking at ergonomic office chairs. My back has been killing me lately and I think my current setup is part of the problem.
I'm sorry to hear about your back pain. Have you consulted a specialist about what might help?
Yeah, I saw a physical therapist last week. She said my posture while working is terrible and recommended a chair with good lumbar support. I sit for hours researching AI ethics papers.
That makes sense. What kind of work do you do exactly?
I'm an AI ethics researcher at a think tank downtown. We're working on a report about algorithmic bias in hiring tools right now.
That sounds fascinating. Must require a lot of focus. Have you considered a standing desk option too?
I have, actually. I tried one for a month but found I missed my urban sketching setup. I've got this little corner by the window where I draw cityscapes on weekends.
Urban sketching sounds like a great creative outlet. Does it help with your back pain at all?
Surprisingly, yes. Standing and moving around while I sketch feels better than sitting at my desk. I'm training for the Chicago Marathon in October, so I'm trying to stay active.
Chicago Marathon! That's impressive. How's your training going with the back issues?
It's been challenging. I've had to modify my long runs and focus more on form. My physical therapist suggested swimming too, which I actually enjoy for the low impact.
